steps: Dissolve 2.0 mmol of corresponding substituted aniline in 5 mL of anhydrous acetonitrile in round-bottom flask, add 0.324 g ( 1.9 mmol ) of 2-chloro-5-(chloromethyl)pyridine and 0.828 g ( 6.0 mmol ) of anhydrous potassium carbonate.
Put a water-cooled backflow condenser on top of the flask and heat the flask under vigorous stirring until the solution starts to boil.
Continue refluxing for several hours. Monitor reaction progress via thin layer chromatography (TLC).
After the reaction is complete, filter off inorganic salts and rigorously wash the sediment with dichloromethane.
Combine all liquid fraction and remove the solvents by evaporation under reduced pressure.
Purify the residue by preparative (TLC) on SiO 2 (eluent EtOAc/hexane 1:2).
creator: Andrii Kyrylchuk
